textByHash.0e75bc1a3ea28eaecca3f4e7c5db426dca84ac88: That doesn't mean it doesn't matter! This isn't just the chance to discover the truth about my ancestor. We also have the chance to expose the treachery of a former king!
textByHash.1047431f6b5fce52e5a1c9bcf068314ab7ab1870: Lizards on the mountain? So it was true! Someone did return from that expedition! Do you think it was Magnus?
textByHash.2c9106ecee2c9692d9f71317f878820ff9211f9d: Maybe. Or maybe someone wasn't telling the truth about that expedition. Regardless, I must find out what really happened. Can you help me?
textByHash.39c5c09299bb9fb023eccce49d15ec89c5920b9f: You came for me! I knew you would!
textByHash.3cfd538e5559d51ef724dfa5fc808fd7d05d95d3: Alas, according to records, the party never returned and were declared to have perished in the field.
textByHash.48230974ae9f4042f71aeed509adfb886c756a95: A few days ago, I received a parcel. There was no name on it and I never saw the person that delivered it. Inside, I found something truly fascinating.
textByHash.49322225474b235f958382bb78de7e87aee42537: If we're to find out what happened on that expedition, we're going to have to retrace its route. And by we, I mean you.
textByHash.51ebb30084660f157ac8d6a12a06aecf117c7443: The name's Phileas Rimor. I was once a soldier in the Shayzien Army but now I'm retired.
textByHash.63095c04313c63c559fd416cf28a3483ac535423: Most of the journal is illegible but I've still managed to find a few readable extracts. Listen to this one...
textByHash.74e51839155e1f2679f3d1c6558833b63cb51e3a: But that's not all. Apparently the journal was written by King Shayzien VII himself!
textByHash.77507049ccab8a2e3254863cf3ed239cdf9932f1: That's one to dwell on later though. For now, we need to work out our next steps.
textByHash.833013440eb4939dd10bc7b03c5ba5b7f59b67ee: Absolutely. We now know for sure that someone returned from that expedition, but we still don't really know why. We need to find out.
textByHash.844919deb4e124e9e1597c61aec53b5e6fe878bc: One day, Magnus was approached by none other than King Shayzien VII himself. The King asked him to lead an expedition to explore Mount Quidamortem in the Kebos Lowlands.
textByHash.84a1d2511a94c0d4030b6c58566fdaef04f70d0a: I'm fine thank you. Thanks for asking though.
textByHash.85e5df08896b2593ca5ffb400145418f3fdd76ae: Is it safe to free me yet?
textByHash.9d5e7b8817dc245db3148968677fa08ecfe19ee7: Hello there. Not very often I get visitors. What brings you here?
textByHash.ab06efb4731f1f223cd053796254fe429ef0c8a0: Well you need to find out more about that expedition. The Library Historical Archive should have records on it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.
textByHash.bc147c8b12d2d12727d23ef2bdc34fa3988754e6: That's a shame. I would have liked to have known what happened to him.
textByHash.c0999c0df173311e7fa9ef60a49c83e4b2daf60e: These barbarians raided my home! They took me prisoner. I thought they were going to kill me!
textByHash.c1a2de0356d84cbf9559806dc923dc9c2deba6e2: Hello again. How is your quest going?
textByHash.d03b3ae1c2e65f0b7312cfd1a1532023b8518ea6: That extract had a date in the 37th decade but the expedition was declared a failure in the 36th decade! If the expedition truly was a failure, why was the King writing about Quidamortem years later?
textByHash.d5408e2af62470ce5b60ce6da25fdb93ea56d6a4: When I was a boy, my grandfather would tell me the story of my ancestor, Magnus Rimor. He was a great explorer who lived a thousand years ago.
textByHash.d79f9773393554d2033c98cb482e0178d8cd836d: Alright. But please, don't take long.
textByHash.d8ce653adccffc2913a952f37af551f8afd5fd64: 'Quidamortem's creatures are compatible. The experimentation is proving successful and we will soon be ready to mass produce.'
textByHash.db1a1e026677ec60e3c793efee08a1386f34d27c: I don't know. I heard one of them saying they'd been paid to do it. But who would pay to have me captured?
textByHash.dd8ef5e93b69c3a3d3beecaa9f318091bddd1b3d: Lord Hosidius? What's he got to do with this? And what about me?
textByHash.f46a74f615479aba93f5b3b870a3dc616be621c0: No. They took it from me. You don't think that's what this was about, do you?
textByHash.f6c0d4de032b23e6708c6c4d659a0dd523d9177a: Well you'll find him in the War Tent in the Shayzien Encampment to the north.
textByHash.f9d7bf507853458c5081abad70166b37fe9ecdb6: We need to find out more about that expedition. The Library Historical Archive should have records on it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.
textByHash.fa9d9e55862a04e4148591013a8f5f32c7594e9d: You know, there is something I wouldn't mind some help with.
textByHash.fdec332120288544f897413e7099c3f49d771f93: There was an old journal, along with a note. The note claims that the journal was stolen from the Tower of Magic during the uprising against Xeric.
textByHash.fe04b7f0769400e68283ed2935b20154e46b8b21: First things first, you'll need to talk to Lord Shayzien. He's ordered that any research work done on the mountain be approved by him first. You'll find him in the War Tent in the Shayzien Encampment to the
